text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members regularly combine their code changes into a shared main branch, often several times a day. Each time new code is added, a CI pipeline automatically starts a process that includes automated builds to compile the software and running tests to check for errors. This approach helps in catching issues early, before they become bigger problems. By fixing bugs immediately after they are introduced, teams can maintain a stable codebase and improve overall software quality. CI pipelines make development faster, more efficient, and less risky.
